xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: Crisil Ltd, Lightbridge IT Park, Saki Vihar Road, Andheri East,Mumbai 400 072, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: Lightbridge IT Park, Saki Vihar Road, Andheri East, Mumbai 400 072
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfContactPerson: 1. Mr. Dinesh Venkatasubramanian (Chief Financial Officer) 2. Ms. Minal Bhosale (Company Secretary)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheCompanyOrLLPOrFirmOfA: DNV(Det Norske Veritas) Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CompanyIDOrLLPIDOrFirmIDOfAsse: DNV(Det Norske Veritas) Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Includes credit ratings such as bond ratings, bank loan ratings and services pertaining to the Global Analytics Centre
Includes Crisil Integral IQ, Crisil Coalition Greenwich and Crisil Intelligence
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Our clientele ranges from micro, small and medium companies to large corporates, investors and top global financial institutions. We also work with commercial and investment banks, insurance companies, private equity players and asset management companies globally. Additionally, we work with policy makers in the infrastructure space in India and other emerging markets.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Group Holding a) S&P India LLC b) S&P Global Asian Holdings Private Limited c) S& P International LLC
Crisil ESG Ratings and Analytics Limited
Crisil Irevna Information Technology (Hangzhou) Co Ltd
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Ethical conduct: Conducting business in fair & ethical manner, bringing transparency in operations, adhering to the laws & regulations and compliance with policies & norms laid down for all the stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Ethical conduct is central to Crisil’s value proposition and recognition as an independent and credible analytical organisation, which has led to continued trust from its clientele
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 26-29 Business Ethics, Transparency & Accountability Ensuring a conflict-free & secure environment Personal Trading Policy Remuneration Policy Taxation Policy Pages 38-39“ Stakeholder Engagement
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Data privacy and information security Identifying & addressing data & associated infrastructure security risks, preventing and responding to cybersecurity incidents & protecting the security and integrity of confidential business information & sensitive/ personal data
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Company has significantly invested in this area over the last few years which will have short term financial impact, but will create positive outcomes in the form of improved security and controls in a long run
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 43-44 Data Privacy, Protection & Security Employee Awareness Pages 106-108 Management Discussion & Analysis Report
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Strong governance oversight Ensuring that the company is effectively managed in the interest of long-term value, through Board structures and practices that facilitate strong governance, effectiveness and accountability
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Strong governance oversight ensures growth and strategic direction
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 22-25 Board and Senior Management Team Board Committees Corporate Information Governance at Crisil Sustainability Governance at Crisil
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Talent retention and succession Attracting and retaining high-quality talent; fair compensation & benefits; enhanced learning & career development of all employees, developing managerial bandwidth and creating a robust succession pipeline
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Talent retention is supported by continuous investment into employee training and upskilling, providing better opportunities for career growth
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 52-56 Workplace Experience Talent Development Culture & Engagement Page 107 Management Discussion & Analysis Report Pages 144-149 Data tables on Human Capital
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Employee enablement and well being Ensuring physical & mental health, safety, well-being, creative freedom, networking opportunity; developing employees’ skills & knowledge; creating a workplace culture that promotes employee engagement, satisfaction and performance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Employee empowerment, learning and development and well-being initiatives lead to a productive workforce
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 54-58 Talent Development Culture and Engagement Wellbeing Health & Safety Pages 51 Skill Building as a strategic lever Pages 144-149 Data tables on Human Capital
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Risk management Identifying, assessing and managing emerging and potential risks facing and posed by the business, including economic volatility, geopolitical shocks, pandemics, uncertain global operating environment, planning effectively for long-term challenges; promoting resiliency; and maintaining organizational flexibility.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk management ensures timely identification of risks and stability of operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 42-43 Risk Management Business Continuity Page 84 Director’s Report - Risk Management Policy Pages 106-108 Management Discussion & Analysis Report
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Innovation and technology Responding to digital trends and emerging technologies; developing and delivering new, market-leading products and services, harnessing data for insights, responding with speed & agility and remaining competitive in the face of market disruption
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Innovation and technological adaptation results in the development of new products and services while ensuring growth by delivering with speed and agility
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 48-50 Digital tools and accelerators AI & Innovation Thought-leadership Pages 60-61 Client relationships Innovative solutions for client needs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Human rights Respecting and upholding the rights and freedoms of all employees, workers and others in Crisil’s value chain, based on principles of dignity, fairness, respect and equality
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Upholding the rights and freedom of all employees, workers and others in Crisil’s value chain ensures ethical behaviour and attracts talent. Policies and practices encourage reporting and ensure due redressal
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 56-59, 64 Culture & Engagement Human Rights Governance around Supply Chain
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Environmental sustainability Making all efforts to reduce emissions, optimize energy, water, paper and waste resources, measures to increase green cover, improve biodiversity and combat climate change.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Climate change can lead to physical risk to Company property due to climate disasters. Further, there is a cost associated with a transition to a low carbon economy
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 72-77 Environmental Policy Energy Conservation Green Building Reducing our GHG footprint Crisil RE Water Conservation Waste Management
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Culture & Engagement Productive employment and decent work for persons from all backgrounds; equal pay for work of equal value
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Creating a global workforce with different perspectives, high engagement and fosters innovation
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Pages 52-53, 56 Global workforce Workplace Experience Culture & Engagement Page 65 Supply chain inclusion
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar:
Crisil’s commitment towards emission-related goals: Being a subsidiary of S&P Global Inc, during 2025 Crisil was covered under S&P Global’s interim SBTi commitment. We continue to have internal emission reduction goals. Visit S&P’s Global’s website to refer to S&P Global’s Impact report
Resource footprint •Maintaining 80%+ solid waste recycling at India locations •Switching to recycled paper •Increasing green cover through plantation •Increasing water conservation efforts
Social •Focusing on investing in future ready talent and digitalisation •Fostering an environment of respect for our workforce encompassing a wide range of backgrounds and perspectives •Improvement of employee engagement •Increasing outreach of the CSR programme, thereby impacting lives of communities positively •Increasing employee volunteering
Governance •Improving employee training and awareness on sustainability •Increasing our local sourcing efforts to support small businesses •Driving sustainability consciousness in the supply chain by increasing social assessments, training and coverage for emissions data •Setting the highest standards of corporate governance

Crisil’s commitment towards emission-related goals: Being a subsidiary of S&P Global Inc, during 2025 Crisil was covered under S&P Global’s interim SBTi commitment. We continue to have internal emission reduction goals. Visit S&P’s Global’s website to refer to S&P Global’s Impact report

Governance •Improving employee training and awareness on sustainability •Increasing our local sourcing efforts to support small businesses •Driving sustainability consciousness in the supply chain by increasing social assessments, training and coverage for emissions data •Setting the highest standards of corporate governance

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible:
Sustainability remained core to opur operations.We reinforced our environmnetal and social commitments,in line with our belief that long-term success must be built responsibly. We achieved a 79% reduction in absolute Scope 1 & 2 emissions,surpassing our Science Based Targets initiative(SBTi)2025 medium-term target of a 25.2% reduction. We expanded green energy adoption,achieved 94% waste recycling cross India operations, and assessed over three-fourths of our suppliers on sustainable practices to ensure responsible and suatinability-chain management. Through Crisil Foundation, we enhanced community programmes in financial literacy and rural resilience to balance growth and positive societal outcomes. Looking ahead, our priorities are clear.We will focus on accelerating revenue growth and driving margin expansion by strengthening our leadership in core businesses. We will also capitalise on high-growth opportunitiies across private markets, mid-tier asset managers and regional banks. Additionally, we will leverage our acquisition of PriceMetrix to drive momentum in wealth benchmarking, and pursue opportunities in new-age sectors, data and analytics, risk-solutions and onulting in India and emerging markets. We will also look to expand our client relationships by scaling AI-enabled products and solutions and building tust through consistent delivery. As we approach the 40th year of making markets function better, our commitment to excellence, innovation and integrity remains unwavering. I would like to thank all our stakeholders for being with us every step of the way.
Amish Mehta Managing Director & CEO Crisil Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: The Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Crisil Limited is responsible for implementation and oversight of the Business Responsibility policy/ies
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: Crisil’s Sustainability Steering Committee, chaired by the MD and CEO, drives the Company’s sustainability agenda. The committee focuses on developing and refining Crisil’s sustainability strategy, setting goals and providing oversight, while monitoring progress to ensure sustainability practices meet global standards. The Board of Directors conducts an annual review of sustainability objectives and implementation plans, which are then communicated to the business units.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfOtherCommitteeFor: Status of compliance with all applicable statutory requirements is reviewed on a quarterly basis by the Crisil Board.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheAgencyIfTheEntityCarr: DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited has provided a “Reasonable Assurance” for Core metrics for Crisil’s Integrated Annual Report based on BRSR (as per SEBI Circular dated July 12, 2023) and provided limited assurance for other non-financial metrics in this Integrated Annual Report including GRI, SASB and BRSR report.

DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited has provided a “Reasonable Assurance” for Core metrics for Crisil’s Integrated Annual Report based on BRSR (as per SEBI Circular dated July 12, 2023) and provided limited assurance for other non-financial metrics in this Integrated Annual Report including GRI, SASB and BRSR report.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Code Of Ethics^, Crisil Values, Data Privacy, Personal Trading Policy, Code of Ethics, Information Security and Risk Awareness, Fostering a Culture of Respect for Managers, POSH, Human Rights

Code of Ethics^, Crisil Values, Data Privacy, Personal Trading Policy, Code of Ethics, Information Security and Risk Awareness, Fostering a Culture of Respect for Managers, POSH, Health & Safety, Skill upgradation, Human Rights
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: Office of the Assistant Commissioner of CGST & Central Tax, Division VIII, Mumbai East
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: Demand of Penalty under the provision of Section 74 (1) of CGST Act, 2017 read with Section 122(2) (b) of the CGST Act, 2017 for alleged disagreement regarding admissibility of credit notes. Appeal is not filed yet. The Company will file an appeal with the appellate authority before the due date of filing appeal which is March 30, 2026
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: Office of the Commercial Tax Officer, Mandaveli Assessment Circle, Chennai South, Tamil Nadu
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: Demand of Penalty under the provision of Section 74 with Rule 142 (5) of CGST Act, 2017 towards GST on Export of Services Demand of Penalty under the provision of Section 74 with Rule 142 (5) of CGST Act, 2017 towards GST on Export of Services
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheCase: Demand of Penalty under the provision of Section 74 with Rule 142 (5) of CGST Act, 2017 towards GST on Export of Services
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: State Appellate Authority (GST Appeals), Mandaveli, South-I
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheCase: Demand of Penalty under the provision of Section 74 (1) of CGST Act, 2017 read with Section 122(2)(b) of the CGST Act, 2017 for alleged disagreement regarding admissibility of credit notes
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: Appeal is not filed yet. The Company will file an appeal with the appellate authority before the due date of filing appeal which is March 30, 2026
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: Crisil’s Code of Ethics, inter-alia, covers prohibition of bribery and corruption. Crisil’s Code of Ethics is available at https://www.Crisil.com/content/dam/Crisil/investors/corporate-governance/code-of-ethics.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkAtAntiCorruptionOrAntiB: Crisil’s Code of Ethics, inter-alia, covers prohibition of bribery and corruption. Crisil’s Code of Ethics is available at https://www.Crisil.com/content/dam/Crisil/investors/corporate-governance/code-of-ethics.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Not applicable as there are no such instances.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse:
Crisil has a comprehensive “Code of Conduct for Directors and Senior Management” available at https://www.Crisil.com/content/dam/Crisil/investors/corporate-governance/code-directors-sr-management.pdf
Every Board member discloses the names of the entities or arrangements in which they are interested which is brought to the attention of the Board.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: With a view to strengthening our efforts in encouraging our suppliers to adopt our Suppliers’ Code of Conduct, our suppliers are trained on Supplier Code of Conduct and awareness on sustainability
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: NA.Sustainability factors are integrated in our products and offerings as sustainability is a strategic and important objective for Crisil. Please refer to Sustainability Offerings on page 63 of the Integrated Annual Report. Since we operate in the service industry, our products and services are not capital intensive and mostly depend on niche data, practical insights and cutting-edge analysis. We frequently invest in IT infrastructure to enhance our client interface and satisfy security and privacy requirements.

NA.Sustainability factors are integrated in our products and offerings as sustainability is a strategic and important objective for Crisil. Please refer to Sustainability Offerings on page 63 of the Integrated Annual Report. Since we operate in the service industry, our products and services are not capital intensive and mostly depend on niche data, practical insights and cutting-edge analysis. We frequently invest in IT infrastructure to enhance our client interface and satisfy security and privacy requirements.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Not applicable. As Crisil is in the services business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence, these issues are not relevant.
Not applicable. As Crisil is in the services business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence, these issues are not relevant.
Not applicable. As Crisil is in the services business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence, these issues are not relevant.
Not applicable. As Crisil is in the services business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence, these issues are not relevant.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableCondu: Not applicable. As Crisil is in the services business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence, these issues are not relevant.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfProductOrService: Not applicable. As Crisil is in the services business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence, these issues are not relevant.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:IndicateInPutMaterial: Nil; Being in the service business, we do not have large spends on input materials, leaving limited opportunities for reuse or recycling. However, we have taken specific initiatives wherever possible, to optimise resources and recycle.97% of office printing in India offices is on eco-friendly paper. Please Refer to tables 27(a) and 27(b, c) on page 155 and 156 of the Sustainability Databook.

Nil; Being in the service business, we do not have large spends on input materials, leaving limited opportunities for reuse or recycling. However, we have taken specific initiatives wherever possible, to optimise resources and recycle.97% of office printing in India offices is on eco-friendly paper. Please Refer to tables 27(a) and 27(b, c) on page 155 and 156 of the Sustainability Databook.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfOtherWaste: Not Applicable. Since Crisil is in the service business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence these issues are not relevant.
Not Applicable. Since Crisil is in the service business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence these issues are not relevant.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:IndicateProductCategory: Not applicable. As Crisil is in the services business, it does not have manufactured products. Hence, these issues are not relevant.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: Crisil has a Policy on Equal Opportunities at the Workplace. Our commitment to maintaining a discrimination-free work environment extends to all persons involved in the operation of the business and prohibits discrimination or unlawful harassment. All employees are responsible for treating others with dignity and respect. The Policy is available at https://www.crisil.com/content/dam/crisil/investors/corporate-governance/crisil-policy-onequal- opportunitiesat-the-workplace.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Employees can reach out to their managers and HR business partners to redress their grievances under terms of the ‘Policy on Redressal of Workplace Harassment’. The mechanism is also applicable to vendors and contractual staff working on Crisil premises.

Employees can reach out to their managers and HR business partners to redress their grievances under terms of the ‘Policy on Redressal of Workplace Harassment’. The mechanism is also applicable to vendors and contractual staff working on Crisil premises.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo:
Details on Business Continuity Planning of Crisil which enables assessing risk and health related hazards. In fiscal 2025, Crisil strengthened its Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCM) framework to focus on resilience, preparedness and regulatory compliance. Through proactive monitoring of global incidents via multiple intelligence platforms, it identified potential risks early, provided timely advisories and enabled informed decision-making. This robust monitoring and response mechanism ensured operational continuity and employee safety in the face of potential disruptions, while elevating organisational readiness worldwide. A thorough review of the BCM programme validated the effectiveness of critical processes, recovery strategies, alternative arrangements and downtime tolerances, ensuring relevance. Regular preparedness exercises, including business continuity drills, evacuation simulations, emergency response team training and employee awareness programmes, were conducted to maintain a high level of readiness.
85% of Crisil employees were trained on Health & Safety measures in 2025. Details of assessment of Crisil offices on Health & Safety. 100% of Crisil locations/offices in India and major offices in London, New York and China were assessed during the year for child labour, forced labour, health and safety practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE:
Integrated Annual Report 2025 100% of Crisil locations/offices in India and major offices in London, New York and China were assessed during the year for child labour, forced labour, health and safety practices.
100% of employees covered for life, health and accident insurance and parental care benefits 1.4% of revenue spent on wellbeing measures
Health & Safety Crisil recognises that a safe workplace is essential to its success and the well-being of its employees, stakeholders and the communities in which it operates Health and Safety Policy Crisil’s Health and Safety Policy addresses the effects of work on employee well-being, encompassing aspects such as ergonomics, fire safety, communicable diseases and travel safety. The policy encourages employee involvement in identifying and mitigating hazards to minimise occupational health and safety risks. Crisil has established standard operating procedures that outline requirements for maintaining safe working conditions and adhering to health and safety protocols.
100% of Crisil locations/offices in India and major offices in London, New York, Poland and China were assessed during the year for health and safety practices.
Health and Safety Training Crisil provides health and safety training to all employees to create awareness within the organisation. Drills, maintenance activities and regular safety stimulations enhance its health and safety programme. In 2025, 85% of its employees were trained in health and safety measures.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa:
Health & Safety Crisil recognises that a safe workplace is essential to its success and the well-being of its employees, stakeholders and the communities in which it operates Health and Safety Policy Crisil’s Health and Safety Policy addresses the effects of work on employee well-being, encompassing aspects such as ergonomics, fire safety, communicable diseases and travel safety. The policy encourages employee involvement in identifying and mitigating hazards to minimise occupational health and safety risks. Crisil has established standard operating procedures that outline requirements for maintaining safe working conditions and adhering to health and safety protocols.
100% of Crisil locations/offices in India and major offices in London, New York, Poland and China were assessed during the year for health and safety practices.
Health and Safety Training Crisil provides health and safety training to all employees to create awareness within the organisation. Drills, maintenance activities and regular safety stimulations enhance its health and safety programme. In 2025, 85% of its employees were trained in health and safety measures.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText: For the table on Performance and carrer development reviews the system is populating total number of employees on roll during the year. However performance and career development reviews are undertaken only at specific timeperiods, typically mid year and year end.Hence the no of employees who are subject to the review will never match up to the number of employees who were on roll during year .Hence the auto population should be discontinued the company should be permitted to enter the total no of employees in the column.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: We contractually bind our major suppliers of IT support, staffing solutions partners, facility management and security services that employ people from the more vulnerable sections with lower literacy levels, to comply with labour standards such as minimum wages, gratuity, bonus, leave, employees’ state insurance and other employment laws.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa:
Supply Chain Sustainability Assessment To ensure that our partners uphold the principles outlined in our Supplier Code of Conduct, we conduct comprehensive assessments of our suppliers. Our evaluation process involves regular self-assessments by suppliers, which cover critical aspects such as: • Environmental responsibilities • Maintenance of safe and healthy workplaces • Safeguarding of human rights throughout their operations
We also focus on essential social regulations, including: • Adherence to Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) regulations • Enforcement of minimum wage standards • Prevention of child labor • Implementation of anti-bribery measures Robust Assessment and Corrective Actions Our assessments are performed regularly, and any unfavorable responses are reviewed by our dedicated business and corporate teams. If necessary, we initiate corrective actions to address any significant gaps and ensure compliance with our Supplier Code of Conduct.
138 suppliers covering 33.06% of our procurement spend assessed for sustainability practices in 2025. 327 suppliers assessed cumulatively, representing 76.59 % of our current year's value of business.
Onboarding New Vendors As of 2025, minimum adherence to assessment completion and adherence to essential social regulations is a criteria used for onboarding new vendors. We believe that by introducing these rigorous standards, we promote ethical practices within our supply chain and enhance the overall integrity and sustainability of our operations. 100% new suppliers were screened for sustainability criteria in 2025
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif:
Crisil has a Stakeholder Engagement Policy, which is accessible at https://www.Crisil.com/content/dam/Crisil/investors/ corporate-governance/CRISIL-Stakeholder-Engagement-Policy.pdf Under this policy, Crisil identifies stakeholders as individuals, groups of individuals or organisations that affect and/or could be affected by/ could impact the company’s activities, products or services and associated performance.
Stakeholder Engagement Policy This policy defines stakeholders as individuals or organisations that influence or are impacted by our activities, products, services, or performance. Stakeholders are identified based on their dependence on Crisil, involvement in financial, social, economic, or environmental matters, ability to impact strategic or operational decisions, and existing or potential legal, commercial, or operational relationships. Our stakeholders include shareholders clients employees, regulators, suppliers and business partners, communities, and the bankers. By recognising these diverse groups, we ensure our engagement processes address their unique needs and concerns, supporting the company’s sustainable growth and responsible business practices.
https://www.crisil.com/content/dam/crisil/investors/corporate-governance/CRISIL-Stakeholder-Engagement-Policy.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta:
Stakeholder consultation and feedback At Crisil, stakeholder consultation and feedback include formal surveys in addition to variety of other means of engagement. The feedback collected,is taken into account and, after due evaluation, is incorporated to improve business processes. Significant learnings may also help shape our strategic initiatives and growth levers. Stakeholders are encouraged to put forth any concerns relating to their engagement with us and reach out to our senior management, if necessary. The engagement scores, grievances and other feedback from stakeholders are monitored at various levels of the management.
Board oversight Crisil’s commitment to stakeholder engagement and governance practices extends beyond mere compliance, with the organisation voluntarily adopting and implementing various initiatives that exceed legal requirements. Every quarter, Crisil Board’s Stakeholder Relationship Committee examines grievances from all stakeholders. Further, once a year, the Committee dedicates time to review learnings from stakeholder surveys and actions proposed, reflecting the importance we attribute to our stakeholder feedback and needs. We also encourage Board and senior management interaction with various stakeholder groups.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn:
Yes. At Crisil, consultation with the stakeholders is important for management of the sustainability attributes in the areas such as governance oversight, employee enablement and well being, stakeholder engagement and sustainability offerings. Our top material issues were identified and prioritized based on their impact on our stakeholders and our business. Such feedback is an important input while devising goals and plans in these areas.
Please refer to page 34- 37 and page 39 of the Integrated Annual Report
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement:
Crisil has a Policy on Equal Opportunities at the Workplace. (Applicable to India locations) We are committed to fostering an environment of respect for all, where all backgrounds are welcomed to drive innovation and serve customers and where we foster a sense of respect for differences and ensuring that learning, development and growth is merit-based. • Details on Supply Chain Inclusion: At Crisil, we provide fair and equal opportunity to all suppliers, regardless of their background or characteristics. Our central procurement team, which is based in India, manages the Company's global procurement initiatives. However, to address specific local requirements of each geographical area, we actively engage with local suppliers wherever possible, ensuring that our procurement practices are tailored to the unique needs of each market. Crisil remains acutely aware of the importance of inclusive procurement strategies, recognising that these approaches can yield broader societal benefits by creating economic opportunities for disadvantaged communities. The supplier policies of Crisil offer guidance on sourcing from these varied groups of suppliers, thereby strengthening our commitment to inclusion in every aspect of our operations. During 2025, our initiatives for inclusive sourcing were as follows: 31.21% of procurement through small and medium enterprises.
• Initiatives for Accessibility The practice of designing and developing resources (physical as well as digital) and technologies that can be used by everyone, including people with disabilities, is called accessibility. Crisil has taken steps to ensure its office premises are physically accessible, employees are aware of accessibility as a concept and select platforms have accessible features.
• Details on Driving Social Change among Local communities: Please refer to page 67 to 71 of the Integrated Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Annual report (4) • Stock exchange intimations (7) • Press releases (2) • Investor meetings (7,1) • Conference calls (4) • Surveys(4)

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherFrequencyOfEngag: Note: reference of frequency of engagement numbers - (1) Ongoing (2) Quarterly (3) Mid-term (4) Annual (5) Weekly (6) Monthly (7) Event-based (8) Planned frequency (9) Project/service-based
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: • Overall payment experience • Accessibility and responsiveness • Long-term and mutually beneficial relationship
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: • Meetings, letters, emails and calls (1) • Mobile applications and portals (1) • Webinars, newsletters and publications(6,7) • Surveys (6) • Feedback forms (9)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherFrequencyOfEngag: Note: reference of frequency of engagement numbers - (1) Ongoing (2) Quarterly (3) Mid-term (4) Annual (5) Weekly (6) Monthly (7) Event-based (8) Planned frequency (9) Project/service-based
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: • Financial awareness, access to formal services and adoption of positive financial practices (1) • Developing cadre of communitybased workers (Sakhi) (1) • MoneyWise Centres for Financial Literacy (CFL) for community under the RBI CFL initiative (1)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherFrequencyOfEngag: Note: reference of frequency of engagement numbers - (1) Ongoing (2) Quarterly (3) Mid-term (4) Annual (5) Weekly (6) Monthly (7) Event-based (8) Planned frequency (9) Project/service-based
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: • Relevant awareness and access to formal financial services • Timely documentation and query resolution through Sakhi cadre and grassroot workers
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI:
Grievance redressal
Crisil provides multiple reporting channels and redressal mechanisms at all levels, enabling employees to report human rights violations. These include: A clear procedure for reporting workplace harassment to the Human Resources team, as outlined in the Redressal of Workplace Harassment Policy A Code of Ethics that explains how to raise concerns, report violations and seek advice Oversight by the Stakeholders’ Relationship Committee of the Board, which regularly reviews policy violations and stakeholder complaints By taking a firm stance on policy violations and reviewing such matters at the highest level, Crisil reinforces a strong compliance culture, demonstrating its commitment to addressing grievances in a fair and timely manner.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons:
Grievance Redressal Crisil has a mechanism for monitoring and addressing complaints related to its code of ethics. It has robust mechanisms in place to raise concerns, seek advice and report violations, if any, with either the reporting manager, the Human Resources department representative, or the Legal or Compliance departments. Employees are also provided with several avenues to raise concerns such as the ethics hotline and the whistleblower email ID in accordance with Crisil’s Whistleblower Policy. Crisil promptly reviews the concerns or violations reported in good faith and takes appropriate action to resolve those issues. Crisil strictly prohibits intimidation or retaliation against anyone who makes a good faith report about a known or suspected violation of code, policy or procedure, or any law or regulation, or who assists with any enquiry or investigation. At the highest level, the Stakeholders’ Relationship Committee of the Board regularly dedicates exclusive time to review policy violations and stakeholder complaints.
Grievance redressal Crisil provides multiple reporting channels and redressal mechanisms at all levels, enabling employees to report human rights violations. These include: A clear procedure for reporting workplace harassment to the Human Resources team, as outlined in the Redressal of Workplace Harassment Policy A Code of Ethics that explains how to raise concerns, report violations and seek advice Oversight by the Stakeholders’ Relationship Committee of the Board, which regularly reviews policy violations and stakeholder complaints By taking a firm stance on policy violations and reviewing such matters at the highest level, Crisil reinforces a strong compliance culture, demonstrating its commitment to addressing grievances in a fair and timely manner.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: Based on the current year assessment, no gaps have been identified necessitating corrective actions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: Please refer to Table 16(b) of the Sustainability Databook on page 147.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: 100% plants and offices were assessed for human rights issues such as child labour, forced/involuntary labour, health and safety practices, working conditions, discrimination at workplace, wages
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT:
To ensure that our partners uphold the principles outlined in our Supplier Code of Conduct, we conduct comprehensive assessments of our suppliers. Our evaluation process involves regular self-assessments by suppliers, which cover critical aspects such as: • Environmental responsibilities • Maintenance of safe and healthy workplaces • Safeguarding of human rights throughout their operations
We also focus on essential social regulations, including: • Adherence to Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) regulations • Enforcement of minimum wage standards • Prevention of child labor • Implementation of anti-bribery measures Robust Assessment and Corrective Actions Our assessments are performed regularly, and any unfavorable responses are reviewed by our dedicated business and corporate teams. If necessary, we initiate corrective actions to address any significant gaps and ensure compliance with our Supplier Code of Conduct.
138 suppliers covering 33.06% of our procurement spend assessed for sustainability practices in 2025. 327 suppliers assessed cumulatively, representing 76.59 % of our current year's value of business.
Onboarding New Vendors As of 2025, minimum adherence to assessment completion and adherence to essential social regulations is a criteria used for onboarding new vendors. We believe that by introducing these rigorous standards, we promote ethical practices within our supply chain and enhance the overall integrity and sustainability of our operations.
100% new suppliers were screened for sustainability criteria in 2025
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple5ExplanatoryText:
Table 1. Details of training to employees (% to total no. of employees in the category) Note: Total headcount is for the entire year, including exits, and hence, will not be comparable with the headcount figures as at December 31 of the respective year. Total headcount should be considered : Permanent :5935 Other than permanent :1622 Total :7557 Hence the % should be : Permanent :5656 /5935:95% Other than permanent :1507 /1622:93% Total :7163 /7557: 95%
Table 2.Details of employees in terms of minimum wages paid the table is taking the total no as denominator ,however the total employee count is global count.Minimum wages concept is applicable fpr India only, so the denominator should be allowed to as per the company , that is for Indian employees only. Note: the above table covers only India employees. Total headcount should be considered : Permanent :2695 Other than permanent :1770 Total :4465 Hence the % should be(more than minimum wages) : Permanent :2695 /2695 :100% Other than permanent :1770 /1770:100% Total :4695 /4695: 100%
Table 3.Details of remuneration/salary/wages Note:Remuneration to MD & CEO has been included in KMP. Aggregate number of employees are not comparable with the headcount as on December 31, 2025, since the aforesaid data pertains to remuneration paid to employees on-roll during 2025. This data pertains to India employees. Excludes remuneration of one board member who was on the board for the part of the year.
Table 6.Details on number of complaints made by employees Note: The above complaints pertain to Crisil Limited on consolidated basis for 2025. For 2024 and 2023 the complaints are on standalone basis for Crisil Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: Independent reasonable external IAR report assurance was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyInCaseA: Independent reasonable external sustainability report assurance was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: Independent reasonable external sustainability report assurance was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyIfAnyIn: Independent reasonable external sustainability report assurance for was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: Independent reasonable external sustainability report assurance for was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc:
Reducing our GHG footprint Despite our relatively low contribution to greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, as part of our environmental program, we track our emissions and implement best practices to minimise our carbon footprint. Being a subsidiary of S&P Global Inc, during 2025 Crisil was covered under S&P Global’s interim SBTi commitment. We continue to have internal emission reduction goals. Our commitment to reducing absolute Scope of 1 and 2 GHG by 25.2% by 2025, compared to the 2019 base year , remains a top priority. In 2025 we were successful and surpassed these mid-term goals and against the target of 25.2% reduction we achieved 79%.
Direct Emissions In 2025, we increased the procurement of green energy for our offices. This contributed to a significant 79% reduction in Scope 1 & 2 emissions in 2025 over 2019. Our diesel generators, used as back-up power, and our fugitive emissions from air conditioning, CO2 fire extinguishers, and mobile emissions from owned cars contribute to our Scope 1 emissions.
Indirect Emissions Indirect emissions accounting covers the following predominant categories relevant to our operations: Purchased goods and services, capital goods, upstream fuel and energy-related activities, upstream transportation and distribution, waste generated in operations, business travel and employee commute Out if these, business travel emission comprises 27.49%. In 2025, we intensified efforts to reduce business travel emissions. We shared regular MIS reports with business teams to raise awareness and identify areas for improvement. Thematic discussions were organised to emphasise the importance of reducing emissions. We also expanded the use of electric vehicles (EVs) for airport transfers and ad hoc business travel, marking a significant step towards responsible business travel and reducing Scope 3 emissions.
We have launched an EV initiative for business travel, encouraging sustainable transportation and reducing emissions. Employees can claim reimbursements for using electric vehicles, promoting behavioural change and a culture of sustainability. Our senior management has also been leading by example by using EVs for business travel.
Additionally, we continued to track and report employee commute emissions, recognising the importance of this category in our overall GHG footprint.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: Independent reasonable external sustainability report assurance for was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Waste Management In 2025, Crisil continued to prioritize responsible waste management. We maintained our rigorous monitoring of both wet and dry waste, ensuring that dry waste was recycled through reputable scrap vendors, while wet waste was processed by certified vendors at designated facilities. Significant reduction in waste sent to landfills, with 94 % of waste generated across Crisil offices in India being recycled in 2025. For more detailed information on waste recovery, please refer to Table 27(a) of the Sustainability Databook. Following the relocation of our Mumbai office in 2025, we experienced an increase in waste generation. We ensured responsible waste management and successfully recycled 94% of our total waste generated. This achievement underscores our commitment to minimizing our environmental footprint and promoting sustainable practices. As part of our ongoing commitment to reducing paper consumption, we expanded our use of eco-friendly printer paper in 2025. Six offices transitioned to eco-friendly paper, and we achieved a significant milestone, with 97% of our office printing in India offices utilizing eco-friendly paper, up from 90% in 2024 and 71% in 2023. We remain dedicated to exploring opportunities to replace traditional printing activities with environmentally friendly alternatives, driving our sustainability efforts forward.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:LocationOfOperationsOrOffices: We do not have operations/offices in/around ecologically sensitive areas.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyIfAnyIn: Independent reasonable external sustainability report assurance for was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: Independent reasonable external sustainability report assurance for was provided by DNV Business Assurance India Private Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSignificantDirectAndI: Not applicable as we do have operations/offices in /around ecologically sensitive areas.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: Business continuity In fiscal 2025, Crisil strengthened its Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCM) framework to focus on resilience, preparedness and regulatory compliance. Through proactive monitoring of global incidents via multiple intelligence platforms, it identified potential risks early, provided timely advisories and enabled informed decision-making. This robust monitoring and response mechanism ensured operational continuity and employee safety, even in the face of potential disruptions, while elevating organisational readiness worldwide. A thorough review of the BCM programme validated the effectiveness of critical processes, recovery strategies, alternative arrangements and downtime tolerances, ensuring relevance. Regular preparedness exercises, including business continuity drills, evacuation simulations, emergency response team training and employee awareness programmes, were conducted to maintain a high level of readiness.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: Supply chain emissions Our efforts continue towards collecting actual emissions data from our supply chain to enhance the accounting for GHG emissions. In 2025, Crisil requested emissions data from suppliers that form top 75% of suppliers by spend. We continued to sensitise and encourage the supply chain to monitor and disclose the carbon footprint of their operations. To further enhance the accuracy of our Scope 3 emissions calculations, we have initiated the collection of Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) data from our suppliers. This move enables us to transition from a spend-based method to a more precise and data-driven approach. By incorporating LCA data, we can better understand the environmental impacts of our supply chain and make more informed decisions to drive sustainability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT:
Water Conservation As the world is facing challenge of dwindling freshwater resources and erratic weather patterns, Crisil remains committed to water conservation and sustainability. Recognizing the importance of preserving this precious resource, we have implemented various initiatives to recycle water and leverage technology to improve water efficiency across our operations. Our water conservation efforts include efficient water management practices, such as using inlet water meters to measure actual withdrawal quantities and estimating discharge quantities based on treatment levels.
Water conservation as part of CSR In 2025, we continued our water conservation efforts through our CSR program, focusing on rural communities in water-scarce regions. We created water harvesting structures in Udaipur and Raigarh, conserving water resources and supporting local ecosystems. Additionally, externally, we protected aquatic ecosystems through beach and wetland cleanup drives, reducing plastic waste and promoting sustainable practices to safeguard water resources. Key highlights of our water conservation initiatives in 2025 include:  Creation of water harvesting structures in waterscarce regions.  Ongoing beach and wetland clean-up drives to combat marine pollution  Continued support for rural communities through our CSR program As part of its CSR program since 2023, Crisil has also focused on conserving water resources for rural communities. During the year, Crisil constructed 4 check dams and rejuvenated 20 wells in water-scarce regions of Rajasthan and Maharashtra. These efforts aim to promote a healthier, more sustainable future.

In 2025, we expanded green energy initiatives to four offices, up from three previously, increasing our renewable energy consumption from 67% in 2024 to 69% in 2025 and further reducing our environmental footprint.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheArea: India offices of Ahmedabad, Gurugram, Pune (one)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI)
Bombay Chambers of Commerce and Industry (BCCI)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfAuthority: There are no instances of adverse orders from regulatory authorities for anti-competitive conduct
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PublicPolicyAdvocated: There were no adverse orders from regulatory authorities on any anti-competitive conduct. Thought Leadership Our thought leadership and outreach initiative is a powerful tool for differentiation through which we develop fresh perspective, showcase deep expertise and lead industry conversations. Industry engagements have elevated Crisil’s position as a credible partner with market leading solutions. Responsible Products In the Ratings business, Crisil reaches out to financial institutions and other investors to create awareness on rating trends. Crisil Ratings and Crisil ESG Ratings bring transparency in service standards through rating scales and rationales, which are published on their website and revised periodically to reflect market dynamics. Crisil Ratings has been a pioneer in publishing Ratings Round-up (since 2000), a semi-annual publication that provides a summary of its rating actions and serves as a leading indicator of the performance of the economy and different sectors for the credit markets. Crisil Ratings has also been publishing annually default and transition rates, since 2004, a benchmark for the quality of ratings helping investors quantify credit risk. Crisil’s internal protocols support the development of responsible, accurate and high-quality products. Appropriate disclaimers included in the deliverables offered by the Company’s businesses vertical ensure that the usage conditions of its offerings are appropriately communicated, reducing the risk of misinterpretation or unintended reliance. Thus, Crisil ensures that every deliverable promotes safe and responsible use across client segments. Operating policies and procedures are subject to internal control checks, including regulatory audits, where applicable. The Company’s processes include receiving feedback and complaints on products and taking corrective actions to ensure continuous improvement. Responsible Public Engagement External stakeholders look up to Crisil as a trusted domain expert and an independent voice. Its engagements may influence policy and outcomes. To ensure the Company’s engagement on public policy matters is conducted responsibly, Crisil has implemented a Framework for Responsible Public Engagement, which defines the principles that guide its policy interactions, its approach and the standards of responsible conduct expected of employees when engaging with regulators, industry or other forums.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: Client publications, Newsletters, media quotes, events, webinars, speakers
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkPublicPolicyPositionAdv: Read our Framework for Responsible Public Engagement [click here to read](https://www.crisil.com/content/dam/crisil/investors/corporate-governance/CRISIL-Framework-for-Responsible-Public-Engagement.pdf)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: In areas where the Crisil Foundation is undertaking long-term CSR projects, an on-ground field team is available at the community level to address and respond to any grievances from the community. This is done either face-to-face within the office premises or over telephonic call — gauging the level of the grievance. In addition, each program has a designated manager from the Crisil Foundation, who periodically monitors and interacts with the teams and beneficiaries to gather feedback and address their queries/ concerns, if any.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText: Table :Details of beneficiaries of CSR projects. Note: * Includes all community individuals who have been directly reached out through Mein Pragati in Assam and Rajasthan. ** Overlapping with beneficiaries reported under S. No. 1 and S. No. 2 *** Covers the Sakhi cadre onboarded, trained and handheld through Mein Pragati in Assam and Rajasthan
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:IntellectualPropertyBasedOnTra: Crisil services do not use intellectual properties from communities based on traditional knowledge.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfAuthorityForIntellectual: Crisil services do not use intellectual properties from communities based on traditional knowledge.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AspirationalDistrictOfCSRProje: Baksa, Barpeta, Darrang, Dhubri, Goalpara, Udalguri
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo:
Each business receives and addresses customer complaints regularly. Complaint redressal is tracked rigorously at various levels of the management. The Stakeholders’ Relationship Committee of the Board regularly dedicates exclusive time to review stakeholder complaints, including customer complaints.
Client Relationships Crisil’s long-standing relationships with its stakeholders are built on trust and engagement. The company has clients with 20+ years of relationships, which is a strong validation of the value of relationship capital created over the years. Meeting client expectations for independent and actionable insights backed by analytical rigor and engaging in responsible and constructive dialogue with market participants is a priority. At Crisil, clients are at the forefront of our commitment to excellence, and their feedback plays a vital role in shaping our services. Crisil actively engages with them through Net Promoter Score surveys, which gauge their satisfaction and provide valuable insights to drive continuous improvement. By integrating client feedback into our operations, the company anticipates and addresses emerging challenges, fostering a trusting relationship that promotes mutual growth and ensures that the company’s offerings remain aligned with their evolving needs.
Responsible Products In the Ratings business, Crisil reaches out to financial institutions and other investors to create awareness on rating trends. Crisil Ratings and Crisil ESG Ratings bring transparency in service standards through rating scales and rationales, which are published on their website and revised periodically to reflect market dynamics. Crisil Ratings has been a pioneer in publishing Ratings Round-up (since 2000), a semi-annual publication that provides a summary of its rating actions and serves as a leading indicator of the performance of the economy and different sectors for the credit markets. Crisil Ratings has also been publishing annually default and transition rates, since 2004, a benchmark for the quality of ratings helping investors quantify credit risk. Crisil’s internal protocols support the development of responsible, accurate and high-quality products. Appropriate disclaimers included in the deliverables offered by the Company’s businesses vertical ensure that the usage conditions of its offerings are appropriately communicated, reducing the risk of misinterpretation or unintended reliance. Thus, Crisil ensures that every deliverable promotes safe and responsible use across client segments. Operating policies and procedures are subject to internal control checks, including regulatory audits, where applicable. The Company’s processes include receiving feedback and complaints on products and taking corrective actions to ensure continuous improvement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePolicyOnCyberSecur: Yes. Crisil also has adopted “Crisil Global Corporate Privacy policy which can be accessed at https://www.crisil.com/content/Crisilcom/en/home/Crisil-privacy-notice.html
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: There were no penalty/ action taken by the regulatory authorities in respect to the aforesaid.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu: Details on products and services offered by Crisil is available at https://www.crisil.com/en/home/ourproduct. html
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo:
Responsible Products In the Ratings business, Crisil reaches out to financial institutions and other investors to create awareness on rating trends. Crisil Ratings and Crisil ESG Ratings bring transparency in service standards through rating scales and rationales, which are published on their website and revised periodically to reflect market dynamics. Crisil Ratings has been a pioneer in publishing Ratings Round-up (since 2000), a semi-annual publication that provides a summary of its rating actions and serves as a leading indicator of the performance of the economy and different sectors for the credit markets. Crisil Ratings has also been publishing annually default and transition rates, since 2004, a benchmark for the quality of ratings helping investors quantify credit risk.
Crisil’s internal protocols support the development of responsible, accurate and high-quality products. Appropriate disclaimers included in the deliverables offered by the Company’s businesses vertical ensure that the usage conditions of its offerings are appropriately communicated, reducing the risk of misinterpretation or unintended reliance. Thus, Crisil ensures that every deliverable promotes safe and responsible use across client segments.
Operating policies and procedures are subject to internal control checks, including regulatory audits, where applicable. The Company’s processes include receiving feedback and complaints on products and taking corrective actions to ensure continuous improvement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: Not applicable considering the nature of Crisil’s business
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableProdu:
Display of product information is not Applicable to Crisil due to the nature of services offered.
Client Relationships Crisil’s long-standing relationships with its stakeholders are built on trust and engagement. The company has clients with 20+ years of relationships, which is a strong validation of the value of relationship capital created over the years. Meeting client expectations for independent and actionable insights backed by analytical rigor and engaging in responsible and constructive dialogue with market participants is a priority. At Crisil, clients are at the forefront of our commitment to excellence, and their feedback plays a vital role in shaping our services. Crisil actively engages with them through Net Promoter Score surveys, which gauge their satisfaction and provide valuable insights to drive continuous improvement. By integrating client feedback into our operations, the company anticipates and addresses emerging challenges, fostering a trusting relationship that promotes mutual growth and ensures that the company’s offerings remain aligned with their evolving needs.
Responsible Products In the Ratings business, Crisil reaches out to financial institutions and other investors to create awareness on rating trends. Crisil Ratings and Crisil ESG Ratings bring transparency in service standards through rating scales and rationales, which are published on their website and revised periodically to reflect market dynamics. Crisil Ratings has been a pioneer in publishing Ratings Round-up (since 2000), a semi-annual publication that provides a summary of its rating actions and serves as a leading indicator of the performance of the economy and different sectors for the credit markets. Crisil Ratings has also been publishing annually default and transition rates, since 2004, a benchmark for the quality of ratings helping investors quantify credit risk. Crisil’s internal protocols support the development of responsible, accurate and high-quality products. Appropriate disclaimers included in the deliverables offered by the Company’s businesses vertical ensure that the usage conditions of its offerings are appropriately communicated, reducing the risk of misinterpretation or unintended reliance. Thus, Crisil ensures that every deliverable promotes safe and responsible use across client segments. Operating policies and procedures are subject to internal control checks, including regulatory audits, where applicable. The Company’s processes include receiving feedback and complaints on products and taking corrective actions to ensure continuous improvement.
Responsible Public Engagement External stakeholders look up to Crisil as a trusted domain expert and an independent voice. Its engagements may influence policy and outcomes. To ensure the Company’s engagement on public policy matters is conducted responsibly, Crisil has implemented a Framework for Responsible Public Engagement, which defines the principles that guide its policy interactions, its approach and the standards of responsible conduct expected of employees when engaging with regulators, industry or other forums.
Innovative Solutions for Client Needs At Crisil, business innovation and client relationships drive growth. The Company’s approach includes developing new client segments, expanding its product portfolio to serve new and emerging client needs and making its offerings more comprehensive. Wealth management segment To scale in the wealth management segment, which was primarily underserved, and extend sector benchmarking capabilities to large global banks and traditional wealth managers, Crisil acquired PriceMetrix in 2025. PriceMetrix is a leading provider of performance benchmarking and data-driven insights for the wealth management industry. With its proprietary database covering $8 trillion in assets under management, PriceMetrix has expanded Crisil’s capabilities in the wealth management and benchmarking space. Data Analytics Data is an asset that requires careful management and governance. During fiscal 2025, our intelligence business vertical helped design and implement robust data strategies for clients. With Crisil’s expertise in data consulting, governance, data engineering and AI, it supported its clients to integrate business, data and technology, with a focus on data quality and facilitate downstream analytics. With extensive experience in the Asia-Pacific and Middle East regions, Crisil possesses a nuanced understanding of the distinct challenges and opportunities each market presents. By providing consulting-led services and using accelerators and point solutions, Crisil supports financial institutions at every stage of their data transformation journey. Integral IQ business Crisil’s Integral IQ business expanded its presence through client diversification and geographical expansion during the year. Crisil Integral IQ business used its deep expertise of having worked in the credit space with large US and European banks to offer solution-driven offerings in credit assessment, model validation and other services to address the mid-size banking segment needs with notable traction in Middle East and Asia. Crisil’s strategy is to provide specialised data and analytics solutions to these clients. Crisil’s commitment to innovation and client-centricity has enabled the company to stay ahead of the curve in addressing evolving client needs. As Crisil continues to navigate the complex and rapidly changing financial landscape, its focus on innovation, client relationships and specialised solutions will remain essential to delivering value and achieving sustainable growth.
